reasoning:
On October 30, the Polymarket estimate for BTC hitting $140,000 by year-end 2025 was 24%, when the price was trading around $109,000 with 62 days remaining. As of November 8-9, Bitcoin's price has declined to approximately $102,300, and there are only 52 days left until the December 31 deadline. 

Using an options-pricing heuristic (touch probability for a barrier option), we can back out an implied volatility of roughly 52-55% from the October 30 Polymarket data. Carrying this volatility forward to today's price of $102.3k and 52 days to expiry, the mathematical probability of hitting a $140,000 high target drops significantly due to both the larger price distance (now requiring a ~37% increase) and time decay. 

The calculated touch probability falls into the 10-14% range. Given that Bitcoin's chart demonstrates a lower-highs retreat from its October $126k all-time high, momentum is currently working against a sharp V-shaped recovery all the way to $140k within a month and a half, facing resistance at $115k and $126k. Therefore, adjusting the 24% base anchor down to 12% provides a structurally sound and well-calibrated forecast reflecting the recent price drop and lost time.
